description: Keeper hooks: (1) events-writer appends one per-pid NDJSON line per hook invocation (lock-free, no SQLite) for the keeperd ingester to fold into the jobs projection; (2) branch-guard is a PreToolUse(Bash) hard-deny that blocks subagents (agent_id present) from creating/switching git branches and from mutating git stash (list/show/create stay allowed) via the deny JSON envelope (still exits 0), keeping worker subagents on the current branch; (2b) escalation-guard is a PreToolUse(Bash) hard-deny that constrains an escalation session (KEEPER_ESCALATION_ROLE env marker set to unblock/resolve/deconflict/repair) to its role's Bash command-family allowlist — denying interpreter one-liners, heredocs, redirects, tee, command substitution, env-runner wrappers, and off-list/compound commands (the file-write bypass class that survives --dangerously-skip-permissions) via the deny JSON envelope; it fails CLOSED for a marked session and stays inert (fail-open) for an unmarked human/subagent session, always exiting 0; (3) sidecar-writer is a fail-open PostToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit|Bash) hook that owns the ~/docs metadata sidecar AND git state — create-or-merge a doc's .yaml on Write, upsert the gist-url on `gh gist create` (NEVER touching the .md), and commit every doc write/edit/delete to the ~/docs repo (pathspec-scoped, mechanical message, fail-open so a commit failure never blocks; still exits 0); (4) docs-pusher is a fail-open Stop hook that pushes ~/docs to its remote once per turn when local is ahead of @{u} (local ahead-count, no fetch), serialized by a .git/ lockfile and best-effort: a non-fast-forward/auth/network failure is logged to .git/keeper-push.log and skipped (never rebase, never --force) and it ALWAYS exits 0 — a Stop hook exiting non-zero would block Claude from stopping; (5) context-hint is a fail-open SessionStart hook that emits one additionalContext line pointing a session at the repo root's CONTEXT.md glossary when it is present and non-empty (absent/empty/unreadable/non-git cwd → emits nothing), a dep-free node-only fs walk that ALWAYS exits 0
